content.problemStatement:
Write a C program to reverse a given string using pointers.


Input Format

The first and only line of input contains a single string SSS - The string which needs to be reversed.


Output Format

Output on a single line, the reversed string.
constraints: S contains only lowercase English letters
content.inputFormat: The first line contains a single string S - The string which needs to be reversed.
content.outputFormat: Output on a single line, the reversed string.

sampleTestCases.explanation: A single-character string remains unchanged when reversed.

solution.approach: The key insight is to use two pointers, one starting from the beginning of the string and one from the end, and swap the characters at these positions. This process is repeated until the two pointers meet in the middle. The algorithm works because it effectively reverses the string in-place, without needing any additional space that scales with input size.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) where N is the length of the string.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) for the C, C++, and Java solutions, as they only use a constant amount of space to store the pointers and temporary swap variable. For Python and JavaScript, the space complexity is O(N) due to the input string being stored.
